title: Gambeya taiensis
intro: Gambeya taiensis is a plant species commonly known as the Thai gamboge tree, a member of the Guttiferae family. The health benefits of Gambeya taiensis include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include xanthones, particularly gambogic acid and gamboge, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gambeya taiensis is often used in traditional herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, to make medicines for the treatment of various health conditions, and it is also used as a pigment in traditional Asian art.
taxonomy: Gambeya taiensis belongs to the family Sapotaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Ericales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Sapotaceae family, Gambeya taiensis is classified as a genus within the group of Sapotes, which are tropical trees known for their edible fruits. The species is also a member of the subtribe Mimusopeae, which includes other species of tropical trees and shrubs. Gambeya taiensis classification is further detailed by its scientific name, which indicates its unique characteristics and distinctiveness within the Sapotaceae family.
uses: Gambeya taiensis is used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is valued for its potential in treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Gambeya taiensis may also be used to treat gastrointestinal problems and to boost the immune system. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Gambeya taiensis in medicinal applications.

It may also exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a subject of interest in the development of new treatments for infections and diseases.
